posts.text:
“Running a school isn’t really like running a business,” says the founder of Apogee Dripping Springs microschool in Texas. “You’re essentially building a community.” 

Learn more about this microschool from Cato’s @ColleenHroncich. 
ow.ly/25wG50Yx389

Join the Cato Institute for a policy discussion on Section 122 of the Trade Act of 1974 and congressional tariff authorities. 

Featuring Cato’s @clark_packard,  Representative @RepJimmyPanetta (D‑CA), @stanveuger of the American Enterprise Institute, and Former Chief of Staff for Representative Ron Kind (D‑WI), @hana_leah. 
  
🗓️ March 26 | 3 PM EDT 
📍 Cato Institute 
  
Register: ow.ly/C6ju50Yx2fH

It is time to face reality: tactical successes in Iran cannot mask what has quickly become another strategic failure in the Middle East, Cato’s @Hoffman8Jon says. 

ow.ly/KIlH50Yx1AY

Moving student loans to the Treasury Department is the biggest step yet toward closing down the Department of Education, writes Cato’s @Gillen_Andrew_J. Taxpayers can expect significant savings from the move. 
ow.ly/Iuhr50YwZUF

Section 702 surveillance collects foreign communications but incidentally captures US gun importers’ emails and calls, creating a hidden supply-chain registry that could violate the Second Amendment and the Firearm Owners Protection Act, explains Cato’s @PGEddington. ow.ly/xCPx50Ywy0f

The Iran war could hit a lot more than oil.

Petrochemicals. Plastics. Aluminum. Semiconductors. Global shipping.

Every day the Strait of Hormuz remains closed, costs rise, inventories deplete, and supply chains tighten.

And Washington is making things worse, @scottlincicome says.

ow.ly/TcMt50YwXfK

“Immigrants are working at way higher rates than the US-born population, which results in more tax revenue being created,” says Cato’s @David_J_Bier. He discussed the benefits of immigration on “Why Is This Happening?” podcast with @chrislhayes. 

➡️ piped.video/watch?v=Uhj5m_98…

Video

New research shows banking deregulation widened access to credit, helping women and minorities start more businesses and close performance gaps with men and whites. Free markets can level the entrepreneurial playing field, says Cato’s @jeffreyamiron.
ow.ly/Q0qM50YwRKo

European leaders are cracking down on online speech. From jailing platform execs to banning VPNs and ending anonymity, they’re blurring the line with authoritarian tactics. 

A warning for the US: don’t follow Europe down a censorial path, says Cato’s @dr_inserra.
ow.ly/87Yy50YwREi

President Trump vowed to fully insure all Persian Gulf shipping. Instead, the administration rolled out a $20 billion reinsurance plan covering only select ships and cargo—a partial, improvised fix far short of his promise, explains Cato’s @DeHavenTad.
ow.ly/pbfN50YwRgG

Trump slammed media coverage of the Iran war as “fake news,” and FCC Chair Carr threatened broadcasters with license loss over “news distortion.” Government policing of the media risks chilling free speech and undermining a free press, say Cato’s @dr_inserra and @bskorup. 

ow.ly/PbwY50YwxVm

Keeping kids safe online is a worthy goal—but good intentions are not enough to make good policy. Recent bills around the KIDS Act and the App Store Accountability Act fail to keep kids safe and threaten online speech for everyone, Cato scholars explain. 
  
ow.ly/A04R50YwxMo

No tax on tips and overtime may sound great, but they add complexity and unfairness to the tax code. We need a simple, fair, and flat tax that would treat everyone the same, says Cato’s @adamnmichel.

Video

Cato’s new media fellow, @RIKKISCHLOTT, joins @MrRBourne to talk Gen Z: how social media shaped them, why online life has made young people both more anxious and more persuadable, and how the socialist left and the alt-right have each found fertile ground. Listen to the Cato Podcast on Apple, Spotify, and our website. 

Apple: ow.ly/rEBC50Ywuug  
Spotify: ow.ly/EH4S50Ywuue  
Our website: ow.ly/Jsm250Ywuuc

SCOTUS has a key opportunity to hear a First Amendment case that could expand access to justice. 

At stake is whether Americans can be punished for offering basic legal advice. Cato’s amicus brief argues the answer is no: the First Amendment protects such speech, even when it involves professional knowledge. 

 ow.ly/ZXPu50Ywot1

The United States is the largest importer in the world. However, according to 2000-2024 data, the US share of global goods import flows has declined. 

The US market is important to other countries, but in most instances, there are other markets where those countries can sell their goods, writes James Bacchus. 

ow.ly/uCgH50Yt1yN

For too long, excessive regulations have forced banks to hold exorbitant amounts of capital as reserves. @federalreserve Vice Chair for Supervision Michelle Bowman announced the Fed’s upcoming proposal to right-size capital requirements at a @CatoCMFA event last week.

Video

The Cato Institute is pleased to announce that author and commentator @RIKKISCHLOTT is joining the Institute as a Cato Media Fellow. 

ow.ly/M7uZ50Ywi7c
